RULING: HEARING REGARDING COURT'S COMPLIANCE WITH ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Pursuant to the October 19, 2018 alternative writ of mandate, the Court hereby vacates its July 17, 2018 order denying Plaintiffs Robert Foster and Laura Foster's motion for trial preference. The Court enters a new order setting this matter for trial preference pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a).

The trial date is February 19, 2019 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is February 28, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 14, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 1, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 15, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than November 29, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Tentative ruling continued, see next entry)
